entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 3 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 3 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-03 07:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Casting a line in a Fairfield paddock, now that’s pretty fly
  • Wrecking ball crashes over to extend lead
  • Before he turns off the lights, Kim Carr has a message for his colleagues
  • $5m program to help jobseekers plug gaps in tourism and hospitality workforce
  • Icon sides with Kyrgios after Tsitsipas furore
  • Queen gets lighter workload as Palace formally winds back her duties
  • Wet weather to continue throughout winter and possibly into summer
  • NSW floods: Residents told to evacuate as Sydney braces for flooding
  • Galle protesters no distraction for Australians
  • COVID vaccine requirement to be scrapped for international arrivals
  • NRL Highlights: Warriors v Tigers - Round 16
  • Dragons open scoring with bizarre try
  • ‘Floor not a ceiling’: Pocock backs 43 per cent 2030 emissions reduction target
  • Russian fury as blasts kill at least 3 in city near Ukraine border
  • Slice of heaven: Warriors too good for Tigers in emotional homecoming
  • Tigers superstar hamstrung: Dustin Martin subbed out of Eagles clash
  • Standing strong: Mass nude photo a stark(ers) display of empowerment
  • Adesanya retains title against Cannonier
  • Case alleging workplace bullying against Auditor-General settled
  • Warriors get first try at home in two years
  • Opposition now claims China is gunning for military base in Solomon Islands

  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Best of cartoons, July 2, 2022
  • Around the world in 40 days: The PM and top ministers’ overseas blitz
  • Motorists should assume petrol price relief ends in September, Chalmers says
  • Sydney's third flood within months
  • Census and the city: Inner Sydney’s wealth is wildly outside the Australian norm
  • Show me your stars: Apartment buyers start pulling deposits after new rating system
  • Sydney’s south-west told to evacuate as ADF, helicopters ready for flood rescues
  • Balmain’s 150-year-old pub to transform into sports, whisky bar
  • Russia attempts to dodge blame for repeated strikes on Ukraine’s civilians
  • ‘So long nerds’: YouTube star Technoblade dies aged 23
  • As COVID hospitalisations soar, France recommends return to masks
  • Rupert Murdoch sent Jerry Hall email to say marriage was at an end
  • Abortion rights rally - Melbourne
  • ‘People are still dying every single day’: Qld hospitals strained once more with COVID
  • Calls to remove mayor after alleged drink-driving crash
  • National Photographic Portrait Prize 2022
  • ‘There’s no guidebook’: What do we expect of a PM’s spouse?
  • The baby bonus generation is starting to turn 18. Has it saved Australia’s population?
  • COVID deaths rise among unvaccinated, unboosted
  • Can we please cut down on the mumsplaining?
  • A life dedicated to the law and justice, without fear or favour
  • Thousands of Australians with COVID are dying, and it will get worse. Are we OK with that?
  • 25 per cent jump in people identifying as Indigenous splits community leaders
  • Divorce applications up as marriages hit the rocks
  • Ash Barty, Lance Franklin and Uncle Jack Charles among winners of the 2022 NAIDOC Awards
  • First case of diphtheria detected in NSW this century
  • Tesla’s sales drop amid supply chain, pandemic problems
  • This artist built his own mausoleum. And it snores
  • Raucous songs and bold characters lift this rock-gig musical
  • I was spellbound by the grindstones – then I found out what they meant
  • NSW government to transform museum into Indigenous cultural space
  • The true-crime podcasts that inspired Liane Moriarty’s latest novel
  • Ian Thorpe hits the water with pals aboard Packer’s gigayacht
  • Torrential rain brings flooding to Sydney
  • Evacuation orders issued in Sydney as floodwaters rise
  • National weather forecast for Sunday July 3
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 3 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Lysychansk ‘on fire’ as two sides dispute control of eastern city
  • A call to arms: Poland’s military and civilian defenses rethink the future
  • On the front lines, Ukrainian women are often the first responders
  • Kyiv disputes Russia’s claim that it has ‘completely surrounded’ Lysychansk
  • She helped get Trump elected. Now she’s raising crypto for Ukraine.
  • London holds vigil for Zara Aleena, another woman slain while walking
  • As Ukraine war bogs down, U.S. assessments face scrutiny
  • A 10-year-old rape victim sought an abortion. A judge urged: Stay pregnant.
  • Tropical Storm Colin brings rain to Carolinas, weakens
  • UN condemns protesters' storming of Libya's parliament
  • Ricky Martin faces restraining order in Puerto Rico
  • Russians press assault on eastern Ukrainian city
  • Palestinians give bullet that killed journalist to US team
  • Macedonians protest French proposals over rift with Bulgaria
  • Israel shoots down Hezbollah drones over Mediterranean
  • Pride parade returns in London on 50th anniversary
  • Greece gets European assistance for summer wildfire season
  • Iran's top diplomat in Syria, slams Israel, Turkish threats
  • One dead after ‘jet truck’ crash at Michigan air show, police say
  • Uvalde schools police chief resigns from city council post
  • Oklahoma plans to execute an inmate nearly every month until 2025
  • Millions of angry bees swarm over highway after semitrailer crash
  • July Fourth travelers face thousands of flight delays, cancellations
  • Florida teen seriously injured by shark bite in holiday attack
  • She died in a Manhattan penthouse but was buried on an island for the poor
  • Jan. 6 showed two identities of Secret Service: Gutsy heroes vs. Trump yes-men
  • N.Y. passes law on guns, starts abortion rights process after Supreme Court rulings
  • Companies offer to help employees seeking abortions. It’ll be tricky.
  • OPEC+ Did Its Job, But Don’t Expect It to Disappear
  • America’s Period Taboo Will Outlast the Tampon Shortage
  • Plastic-Munching Bacteria Offer Hope for Recycling
  • This was the term the Supreme Court decided to ignore the speed bumps
  • Congress’s big China bill must pass — but with strings attached
  • We can no longer avoid a criminal investigation into Donald Trump
  • Voters in Virginia’s 7th District should be wary of Yesli Vega’s ignorance
  • A new Supreme Court case threatens another body blow to our democracy
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 3 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 3 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-03 06:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong leader John Lee vows to reduce daily caseloads, lobby mainland Chinese officials for ‘limited measures’ to reopen border
  • Thousands show up for grand opening of Hong Kong Palace Museum to see 900 precious relics and national treasures on loan from Beijing
  • Hong Kong Sevens must be a proper comeback party, or we should wait
  • Can Hong Kong’s Park Lane shopping strip come back to life? Owners hope adding food, drinks will help
  • Typhoon Chaba: No 3 warning signal downgraded to No 1, squally showers still expected over Hong Kong
  • Confucius, the opera: Hong Kong composer Gustav Mak mixes Western, Chinese elements in three-hour tribute to sage
  • Hong Kong’s new youth minister vows to quickly create blueprint to help disaffected young people, but admits progress will not be easy
  • Xi Jinping’s meet-and-greet with Hong Kong’s elite set tongues wagging on pecking order of political big guns and second-generation tycoons
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong reports 2,227 infections even as bad weather forces testing centres to close, while Macau confirms 56 cases as outbreak persists
  • Covid-19: new cases on the rise in China as travel rules ease
  • End of ‘Angola model’ sees number of Chinese in oil-rich African country plummet
  • Alibaba forms new data intelligence services subsidiary to deepen e-commerce giant’s pivot into enterprise market
  • How the crackdown on China’s top influencers is shaking up the once-booming live-streaming e-commerce industry
  • Could Chinese demand soften the blow of a US recession on Hong Kong, South Korea and Singapore?
  • Chinese ride-hailing giant Didi still awaits final ruling from Beijing, one year after it was put under cybersecurity review
  • Tesla’s second quarter sales drop amid supply chain issues and China pandemic restrictions
  • PLA missile interceptor test seen as response to warning from Taipei
  • Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi seeks to bolster Beijing’s position in Southeast Asia on latest tour of region
  • China accuses NSA of FoxAcid hack attack on science research groups
  • China eases Covid entry restriction to allow transit via third country
  • Meet the Chinese fish robots that can ‘eat’ microplastics – and maybe help clean up oceans
  • Hong Kong at 25: where mainland ‘drifters’ have made their home
  • Mystery over purpose of China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation’s new lab
  • China calls on regional bloc to ‘oppose hegemonic bullying’ as Nato set to expand
  • China’s internet watchdog ramps up crackdown on online scams amid growing number of counterfeit financial services apps
  • Airbus sells 292 A320 aircraft to four Chinese airlines in a blow to Boeing, as US-China tension tips balance in European maker’s favour
  • Set in Bali, filmed in Australia: George Clooney, Julia Roberts film Ticket to Paradise sparks ‘colonial gaze’ debate
  • India’s war on meat could be critical for PM Modi’s BJP in the 2024 election
  • Awkward Boris Johnson-Jacinda Ardern handshake goes viral: ‘It looked like he was trying to tear her arm off’
  • Malaysia’s PM Ismail Sabri Yaacob faces mounting pressure to call snap vote, as Najib and allies step up Umno faction war
  • Malaysia’s Mahathir proud Malaysia-born Australian is Canberra’s foreign minister
  • Roe v Wade: Indian women fear ‘ripple effect’ after US abortion law struck down
  • Rock star Randy Bachman of the Guess Who, and Bachman-Turner Overdrive reunited with stolen guitar in Japan after 45 years
  • Microplastics found in humans’ lungs, kidneys, spleen, placenta: ‘They’re everywhere’
  • Zelensky calls for international help to help rebuild Ukraine after war with Russia
  • Macedonians protest against French proposals for solving EU rift with Bulgaria
  • Afghan clerics’ assembly urges recognition of Taliban government
  • Israel says it has shot down Hezbollah drones headed for Mediterranean gas rig
  • Ukraine mayor accuses Russia of using banned cluster bombs, killing four
  • Thousands sign petition demanding removal of Supreme Court judge after Roe vs Wade abortion ruling
  • Jerry Hall files for divorce from Rupert Murdoch, asks for spousal support
  • Pride parade returns in London on 50th anniversary of first parade
  • Iran earthquakes kill five, injure more than 80, with village near epicentre ‘destroyed’
  • Trump documentary maker ‘has armed guards for protection’ after giving evidence to January 6 committee
  • US: Millions of bees escape on Utah road after truck crash; driver, officers stung
  • Musk breaks longest Twitter silence in four years when he meets Pope Francis
  • Hong Kong’s dry spell for new stock offerings set to end with big China IPO deals
  • Nio, XPeng and Li Auto reported healthy sales of electric cars in June as they emerge from Covid-19-induced disruption
  • Climate change: Hong Kong bus operator KMB seeks government support to power rapid conversion of fleet to electric vehicles
  • Change shouldn’t intimidate Hong Kong – we have always thrived on it
  • How Hong Kong craft brewery became a passion project for co-founder Scott Powrie, and his ambition to climb some of the world’s highest peaks
  • How Hong Kong basketball star playing in US has changed in just 1 year – she now exudes self-confidence and gets much more sleep
  • Where ‘plastic’ came from, its crafty origins and how it became the dirty word it is today
  • Why trans women opt for gender confirmation surgery in Thailand whatever the cost, when procedures can be much cheaper, or free, at home
  • The man who can answer any question on China you might have, and why ‘Have you looked in Wilkinson?’ will remain the common response to a Chinese historical query
  • Fallen Angels: Wong Kar-wai on casting Leon Lai, Michele Reis, Charlie Yeung and Karen Mok against type in 1995 film
  • How to sleep well: have a dog in your room, share your bed with a partner (but not the dog). And amid the pandemic, expect weird dreams
  • The hyaluron pen, a TikTok fave, gives you fuller lips at home – but experts warn it’s not safe
  • Netflix K-drama Cafe Minamdang: lively shaman-themed drama series starring Seo In-guk and Oh Yeon-seo is hit and miss
  • 8 most romantic restaurants in Hong Kong: from Michelin-starred Écriture and Tirpse, to knockout views atop the ICC at Odyssée and Tosca di Angelo sure to dazzle any date
  • How Chinese crypto king Changpeng Zhao lost US$77.5 billion: the Binance CEO went from boom to bust amid the bitcoin and NFT crash, but luckily doesn’t ‘really care much about money’
  • Meet Karan Adani, son of Asia’s richest man Gautam Adani: the 35-year-old CEO of APSEZ is set to inherit Adani Group and has been ‘groomed’ for his dad’s multibillion-dollar empire since 2009
  • Meet Elvis Presley’s ‘stepson’ Navarone Garibaldi: he’s Priscilla’s son with her second husband and Lisa Marie’s half-brother and only sibling ... and also a singer in a rock band
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 3 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Mitsui OSK chief says Japan has no choice but to buy Russian gas

  • Covid vaccines: how can immune imprinting help experts to rethink jabs?
  • Help truly wanted, even if you’ve been in jail
  • Scrutiny of ESG claims for private investments grows
  • Chinese hackers kept up hiring drive despite FBI indictment
  • Fears of recession leave €40bn of European corporate bonds in distress
  • Volatility and gambling reform toughen the odds for 888 bond deal
  • US energy stocks buck dismal trend with ‘massive outperformance’
  • Joe Biden’s lofty climate ambitions are brought down to earth
  • Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows files for US bankruptcy
  • US travellers warned of flight chaos as holiday weekend demand soars
  • Humanities degrees: ave atque vale
  • Labour shortfall leading to ‘catastrophic’ food waste, UK farmers warn
  • An independence referendum should be the last thing on Sturgeon’s mind
  • War excites speculation about the possible break-up of Russia
  • Levelling up with three top London tennis coaches
  • Asia-Pacific remains stuck on coal for half its power needs

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 3 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Osaka firm head relocates offices for employee safety after 3-11
  • ‘PlayStation’ pioneer Kutaragi named faculty dean at Kindai University
  • Glitch at KDDI disrupting smartphone calls, internet use
  • Ukraine evacuees serve up home cooking at Tokyo restaurant
  • LDP lawmakers receive pamphlet that says being gay is ‘disorder’
  • U.S. investment firm gets 1st refusal right over Sogo, Seibu sale
  • 7 overseas IT firms face fines for failing to register in Japan
  • Russia’s shock LNG move raises fears of further electricity crunch
  • China to loosen entry restrictions on U.S. citizens, transit via 3rd country now permitted
  • North Korea slams US-South Korea-Japan military cooperation
  • China’s top diplomat visits Myanmar amid opposition protest
  • New mite species named after how they were found via social media
  • EDITORIAL: Japan, S. Korea miss golden opportunity to put ties on track
  • VOX POPULI: Putin’s decree to ‘seize’ control of Sakhalin-2 is bad news for Japan
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
